steps.title: Identify the sentence structure
steps.content: The phrase “assuming solar panel installers' cooperation” is set off parenthetically. The opening dash appears after “that—” (before “assuming”). The punctuation after “cooperation” must close this parenthetical insertion, leaving “this practice has the potential to increase…” as the main clause.

steps.title: Apply the paired-dash rule
steps.content: A dash correctly closes the parenthetical phrase, completing the pair. The rule is: dashes work in pairs to set off parenthetical or introductory elements. A comma (A) doesn't properly mark the end of a parenthetical insertion; a colon (C) would incorrectly introduce an elaboration; and no punctuation (D) leaves the parenthetical unclosed. This matches choice B — B is correct.
